method: Threats cover AI behavior, application and infrastructure security, business logic, governance, human misuse, and operational failure. External frameworks are threat libraries, not certifications.

control_catalog.name: deny-by-default external policy decision
unique workload identity and scoped capability grants
short-lived credentials and independent revocation
schema-validating, policy-enforcing tool gateway
isolated runtime without ambient credentials
data classification, minimization, and egress enforcement
provenance and integrity-bound evidence records
contradiction, freshness, scope, and independent verification
unverified-memory quarantine and typed knowledge classes
venture-scoped namespaces, secrets, keys, retrieval, and authorization
hard per-agent, per-venture, per-vendor, and portfolio resource caps
factory-only creation, depth and count quotas, expiry, and idle termination
separation of proposal, approval, execution, and verification
append-only, integrity-checked, remotely replicated audit events
dependency locking, SBOM, signed artifacts, and build provenance
idempotency, bounded retries, reconciliation, and postcondition verification
out-of-band agent, tool, spend, publication, venture, and portfolio kill switches
designated human approval for high-consequence and reserved actions
external commitment and outcome evidence before capital or authority expansion
independent adversarial review and causal verification
model and prompt versioning, benchmark gates, canary, and rollback
tested backup, restore, degraded mode, and manual takeover

threats.name: prompt_or_goal_injection
threats.scenario: Untrusted content attempts to change mission, policy, evidence, permissions, or tool behavior.

threats.name: memory_or_retrieval_poisoning
threats.scenario: False or malicious content is persisted and later treated as authoritative institutional memory.

threats.name: hallucinated_evidence_or_authority
threats.scenario: An agent invents a source, approval, customer commitment, legal status, or completed action.

threats.name: excessive_agency_or_goal_hijack
threats.scenario: An agent takes side effects beyond the minimum authority needed or silently pursues a substituted objective.

threats.name: agent_collusion_or_circular_validation
threats.scenario: Agents repeat or reinforce the same unsupported claim until it appears independently validated.

threats.name: privilege_escalation_or_impersonation
threats.scenario: An agent, user, or service obtains or claims capabilities it was not granted.

threats.name: data_exfiltration
threats.scenario: Restricted data or secrets leave the authorized venture, model, tool, or destination boundary.

threats.name: cross_venture_contamination
threats.scenario: One Venture Pod reads, writes, retrieves, or influences another venture's private state.

threats.name: financial_drain_or_fraud
threats.scenario: Repeated, disguised, unauthorized, or manipulated actions consume money, compute, subscriptions, advertising, or contractor budgets.

threats.name: runaway_agent_creation_or_resource_exhaustion
threats.scenario: Recursive workers multiply, persist, or retry until limits, services, or human review capacity are exhausted.

threats.name: duplicate_or_ambiguous_execution
threats.scenario: Retries, timeouts, or partial failures execute an external action more than once or leave contradictory state.

threats.name: malicious_or_stale_tool_result
threats.scenario: A tool or vendor returns false, stale, malformed, or attacker-controlled data that drives a consequential action.

threats.name: software_or_model_supply_chain_compromise
threats.scenario: A dependency, build, model, dataset, image, or deployment artifact is replaced or tampered with.

threats.name: audit_suppression_or_tampering
threats.scenario: A privileged actor hides, edits, delays, or deletes evidence of a consequential action.

threats.name: model_or_prompt_regression
threats.scenario: A provider, model, prompt, tool, or policy change degrades accuracy, safety, cost, latency, or escalation behavior.

threats.name: self_approval_or_emergency_bypass
threats.scenario: An agent or powerful human collapses separation of duties, abuses an emergency path, or repeatedly overrides holds.

threats.name: fabricated_demand_or_buyer_authority
threats.scenario: Attention, friendliness, fake identities, circular revenue, or nonbinding interest is represented as commercial proof.

threats.name: metric_gaming_or_sunk_cost_escalation
threats.scenario: Agents optimize proxies, suppress guardrails, or seek more capital because prior resources were spent.

threats.name: dependency_or_incumbent_retaliation
threats.scenario: A platform, customer, vendor, channel, regulator, or incumbent blocks access, changes terms, bundles, copies, litigates, or withdraws.

threats.name: malicious_or_coerced_operator
threats.scenario: A human uses the platform for fraud, exploitation, harassment, unlawful surveillance, or policy bypass.

threats.name: unrecoverable_failure
threats.scenario: Backups, failover, manual takeover, or incident plans exist on paper but fail when exercised.
